title: Use of Let
tagline: दूसरों को करने दो, खुद को करने दो — 'Let' सीखो!
summary: Day 13 में आप 'Use of Let' सीखेंगे — English का एक बेहद practical और ज़रूरी grammar structure। 'Let' का उपयोग तब होता है जब आप किसी को कुछ करने की permission देते हैं, या खुद कुछ करने की request करते हैं। Formula है: Let + object pronoun (me/him/her/us/them/it) + base verb। यह structure रोज़मर्रा की बातचीत, office meetings, interviews, और formal writing — सब जगह use होता है।
hindiSummary: आज का topic है 'Use of Let' — जिससे आप सीखेंगे: 'Let me' (मुझे करने दो), 'Let him/her' (उसे करने दो), 'Let us' (हमें करने दो), 'Let them' (उन्हें करने दो), और 'Don't let' (मत करने दो)। यह structure simple है लेकिन बहुत powerful है — और इसके साथ सबसे आम गलती है 'to' लगाना (जैसे Let me to go — यह गलत है) या subject pronouns use करना (Let he go — यह भी गलत है)।
whatYouWillLearn: Core formula: Let + object pronoun + base verb
'Let him/her' — giving permission to a third person
'Let us' — formal permission for a group
'Let them' — allowing a group to act
Negative form: 'Don't let' + object + base verb
Question forms: Will you let me...? Should I let him...?
Common mistakes: using 'to', using subject pronouns, using '-ing' form
Advanced use: 'Let go of', 'Let alone', 'Let it be'
Contrast: Let vs Allow vs Make vs Have vs Get

letCheatSheet.objectPronouns: me / him / her / us / them / it

examples.letMe: Let me explain. / Let me try.
examples.letHim: Let him go. / Let him present.
examples.letHer: Let her speak. / Let her lead.
examples.letUs: Let us begin. / Let us review.
examples.letThem: Let them decide. / Let them play.
examples.dontLet: Don't let him in. / Don't let it slip.
examples.questionForm: Will you let me try? / Should I let him come?

commonMistakes.wrong1: Let me to go → Right: Let me go
commonMistakes.wrong2: Let he go → Right: Let him go
commonMistakes.wrong3: Let her going → Right: Let her go
commonMistakes.wrong4: Let not him in → Right: Don't let him in

advancedUse.letAlone: I can't drive, let alone fly.
advancedUse.letItBe: Let it be — don't force it.
advancedUse.letKnow: Let me know if you need help.
advancedUse.letSee: Let me see what I can do.

motivationalQuote: Permission is the first step to action. Learn to say 'Let me' with confidence, and watch doors open for you!
